ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic bagel entry (FOODON_03315763); no specific double-cheese-bagel entry exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers bagels generically; no double-cheese variant code exists. Closest parent match used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for double cheese bagel; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one double cheese bagel of approximately 100 g, consisting of a baked bagel base generously spread or filled with two layers of cream cheese or processed cheese.

nutrient_source.title: Double Cheese Bagel — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Double Cheese Bagel (cr_id G00011075). All per-100g values are sourced from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a double-cheese bagel. GI of 69 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for plain white bagel (GI ~69, medium category). The high fat and protein content from double cheese substantially blunts the effective glycaemic response in practice; however, the structural GI of the bagel base is used as the conservative reference. GL per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (69 × 8.85) / 100 ≈ 6.1 for a 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 1279 mg per 100 g — more than double the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this food; a single 100 g serving delivers approximately 1279 mg sodium, which exceeds the entire recommended daily sodium limit for hypertensive patients and provides no practical modification pathway to reduce sodium to a safe level.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low net carbohydrate (8.85 g/100 g) with high protein (16.09 g/100 g) and fat (23.06 g/100 g) blunts glycaemic response, but high saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g) impairs insulin sensitivity with regular consumption
type2_diabetes.guidance: The low carbohydrate content and high protein-fat ratio produce a moderate glycaemic impact suitable for occasional consumption by people with Type 2 Diabetes, but the high saturated fat load from double cheese should be monitored carefully as it worsens insulin resistance over time; portion control and infrequent consumption are advised.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~12.5 g/100 g) and critically elevated sodium (1279 mg/100 g) both independently elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: People with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid this food due to the combined burden of high saturated fat, which raises LDL cholesterol, and critically high sodium, which elevates blood pressure; regular consumption is strongly contraindicated for cardiovascular health.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat ~12.5 g/100 g from dairy cheese promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine pathways; high sodium (1279 mg/100 g) worsens joint tissue swelling
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this food; the high saturated fat from double cheese drives pro-inflammatory cytokine activity and the critically high sodium exacerbates joint inflammation and fluid retention.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Double Cheese Bagel is a Singaporean café-style dish consisting of a baked wheat bagel base generously layered with two forms of cheese — typically cream cheese and a processed or sliced cheese — resulting in a rich, dense, and savoury snack or light meal. The bagel base provides a chewy, slightly dense texture with a mild yeast flavour, while the double cheese topping contributes a creamy, salty, and tangy profile. Per 100 g, the dish delivers 307 kcal, 16.09 g protein, 23.06 g fat (approximately 12.5 g saturated), and only 8.85 g carbohydrate, making it a high-fat, moderate-protein, low-carbohydrate food. Sodium is critically high at 1279 mg/100 g, driven by the salt content of both cheese layers and the bagel itself. Calcium is exceptionally high at 1375 mg/100 g, reflecting the dense dairy content. Clinically, this food is unsuitable for hypertension and cardiovascular disease management due to its sodium and saturated fat load. For Type 2 Diabetes, the low carbohydrate content is favourable but the saturated fat burden requires monitoring. It is also contraindicated for rheumatoid arthritis due to pro-inflammatory saturated fat and high sodium. The dish is popular as a breakfast or lunch item in Singaporean cafés and fast-food outlets.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in a cheese bagel?
Can diabetics eat a cheese bagel?
Is cheese bagel good for high blood pressure?
Bagel with cream cheese nutrition Singapore
Is double cheese bagel suitable for heart disease?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry for Double Cheese Bagel (cr_id G00011075) with a stated confidence of 0.95. The source is an approved primary regional source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are present and internally consistent.
notes: dietary_fibre_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this is a confirmed zero per the HPB source for this specific product formulation — the refined bagel base and cream cheese contribute negligible dietary fibre. saturated_fat_g of approximately 12.5 g/100 g is estimated from the total fat (23.06 g/100 g) and the known fatty acid profile of cream cheese and processed cheese; the HPB record does not separately report saturated fat, so this is an internally derived estimate consistent with USDA FoodData Central data for cream cheese (USDA SR Legacy #01017, saturated fat ~20 g/100 g for cream cheese) weighted against the bagel base. The following fields are set to -1 because detailed data for these nutrients is not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this specific Double Cheese Bagel composite (cr_id G00011075) as of 2026-04-16, and no supplementary data for this exact composite was found in USDA FoodData Central or FAO/INFOODS SEA: monounsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB for this composite dish; polyunsaturated_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractionation not reported by HPB for this composite dish; trans_fat_g — trans fat not separately reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_c_mg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_a_mcg_rae — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_b3_niacin_mg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_b6_mg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_b12_mcg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; folate_mcg_dfe — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_d_mcg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; vitamin_e_mg — micronutrient not reported by HPB for this composite dish; phosphorus_mg — mineral not reported by HPB for this composite dish; zinc_mg — mineral not reported by HPB for this composite dish; selenium_mcg — mineral not reported by HPB for this composite dish; water_g — moisture content not reported by HPB for this composite dish. Sources consulted that confirmed unavailability of these values: HPB Singapore Foodo database (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/, accessed 2026-04-16) and USDA FoodData Central (https://fdc.nal.usda.gov/, accessed 2026-04-16). GI of 69 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database plain white bagel reference; the actual GI of this specific product has not been independently measured.
